name: simple_model
positioning: Turn macros from safe codemod units into a deterministic project-optimization substrate: discoverable from semantic graph motifs, composable into verified plans, executable through bounded search, and self-improving through benchmark evidence.
principle: The hard advantage is not writing more macros by hand. The hard advantage is making macros behave like typed, testable, reversible optimization operators over a semantic project graph.
why_this_can_be_hard_for_others: Most coding agents treat code changes as conversational edits; simple_model already has struct, semantic graph, risk gates, macro contracts, rollback, benchmark scorecards, and plugin command surfaces.

Most codemod systems are strong at known migrations but weak at project-specific discovery. simple_model can derive macro candidates from adoption gaps, graph motifs, dynamic edges, tests, ownership, and score deltas.

Most static-analysis tools stop at findings. simple_model can turn repeated findings into review-only macro candidates, prove preconditions, run drills, and route safe subsets into deterministic apply queues.

Most AI workflows do not preserve replayable evidence. simple_model can attach graph paths, parser confidence, run logs, rollback manifests, affected tests, and release SLO outcomes to each macro decision.
north_star: Given a messy large repository, simple_model should discover structural improvement patterns, synthesize typed macro operators, search for the best macro plan under risk and runtime budgets, prove safety through drills and tests, apply only evidence-sufficient changes, and learn which macro families work across projects.

macro_power: Macros are typed operators with inputs, outputs, graph preconditions, write-set effects, score effects, rollback semantics, and affected-test selectors.
Macro candidates can be generated from graph motifs and repeated findings without becoming apply-capable until fixtures, proofs, and policy gates pass.
Multiple macros can be composed into plans only when their read/write sets, graph effects, dynamic risks, and rollback scopes are compatible.
The optimizer can search macro plans under budget and explain why selected plans dominate alternatives.
production_safety: Every apply path has dry-run, precondition proof, conflict analysis, idempotency drill, rollback drill, affected-test proof, and replay log.
Unsafe dynamic edges, low parser confidence, missing tests, or ownership conflicts automatically downgrade actions to review-first or gather-evidence.
Macro plan execution is transactional: partial failure yields deterministic rollback and a resumable run record.
Release gates include false-safe-apply, rollback failure, non-idempotency, missed affected tests, and benchmark regression thresholds.
learning_loop: Macro outcomes feed a local corpus with success/failure, score delta, runtime, review reason, rollback cost, and changed graph motifs.
The system can rank macro families by observed value across fixtures and local repos.
Generated macro templates are promoted only after crossing deterministic evidence thresholds.
Reports show which macro families are not worth automating yet.

waves.name: Typed Macro Operator Algebra
waves.objective: Define macros as first-class optimization operators with graph effects, type constraints, conflict rules, and proof obligations.

waves.name: Macro Discovery And Synthesis
waves.objective: Generate high-quality macro candidates from semantic graph motifs, repeated findings, benchmark labels, and repository-specific drift patterns.

waves.name: Compositional Macro Planner
waves.objective: Search, rank, compose, and execute macro plans under deterministic safety, risk, runtime, and rollback constraints.

waves.name: Macro Outcome Learning
waves.objective: Use local evidence to rank macro families, promote candidates, reject weak automations, and feed release-grade benchmarks.

waves.name: Macro Product Surface
waves.objective: Make the plugin expose macro intelligence as a guided workflow: discover, prove, plan, simulate, apply, rollback, and report.

todos.task: Discover macro candidates from semantic graph motifs such as boundary drift, orphaned exports, duplicated routes, stale generated clients, missing tests, ownership splits, and dynamic evidence gaps.
acceptance: Discovery consumes Semantic Graph v2.1, dynamic edges, parser tiers, symbol identity, and test impact DAG.
Each motif candidate includes graph path evidence, confidence, expected operator family, missing proof obligations, and review reason.
Candidates are clustered by repeated structure rather than file-name heuristics.
Low-confidence motifs are emitted as gather-evidence actions, not apply-capable macros.

todos.task: Create a macro-dominant initialization workflow for taking over a large half-built project: diagnose, ingest, tier parsers, build semantic graph, discover macro motifs, generate initialization steps, and isolate the few AI leaf tasks.
acceptance: Workflow produces ordered initialization phases with deterministic commands and artifacts.
Macros own structural decisions; AI tasks are emitted only as bounded leaf clarifications with explicit inputs and outputs.
Initialization report includes readiness, blockers, macro-safe actions, review actions, evidence gaps, and next commands.
Plugin exposes takeover-init as a read-only command for external repositories.

todos.task: Generate interface stability commitments for half-built projects by combining interface scans, semantic graph evidence, dynamic edges, macro plans, and explicit AI leaf-review requests.
acceptance: Commitment classifies interfaces as stable, provisional, experimental, deprecated, or blocked with evidence.
Breaking-change policy, compatibility window, owner/reviewer requirements, and affected tests are emitted per interface group.
Macro recommendations dominate remediation; AI tasks only request missing intent, owner, or compatibility decisions.
Release SLO and takeover init can consume the stability commitment artifact.

todos.task: Expand benchmarks into a macro gauntlet that tests operator discovery, synthesis, composition, transaction execution, rollback, and promotion across adversarial repo fixtures.
acceptance: Gauntlet includes safe, unsafe, ambiguous, dynamic, generated-code, conflicting-write, stale-test, and ownership-conflict cases.
Metrics include discovery recall, false-safe-apply, composition rejection accuracy, transaction rollback success, runtime, and score delta.
Gauntlet can run offline and produces deterministic JSON/Markdown scorecards.
Release SLO includes macro gauntlet thresholds.

todos.task: Upgrade adoption cockpit into a macro cockpit that guides discover, prove, compose, simulate, apply, rollback, and promote workflows.
acceptance: Cockpit shows discovered motifs, synthesized templates, safe plans, blocked plans, proof bundles, outcome rankings, and promotion status.
Terminal output remains concise with top safe actions, top review actions, top evidence gaps, and top retired macro ideas.
HTML report is static and local-only.
Plugin exposes macro-cockpit as a read-only command by default.

todos.task: Analyze whether multiple macro operators can be safely composed by checking read/write conflicts, graph-effect conflicts, dynamic risk escalation, test coverage overlap, and rollback scope compatibility.
acceptance: Composition rejects conflicting write sets, incompatible graph effects, unbounded rollback scopes, and unsafe dynamic dependencies.
Independent operators can be grouped into parallel stages with deterministic ordering.
Composition output includes accepted groups, rejected pairs, conflict reasons, and required affected tests.
The scheduler can consume composition stages as task input.

todos.task: Add an external-repo macro advisor that tells users exactly which macro workflows are worth running in their project and which need more evidence first.
acceptance: Advisor consumes external eval, parser tiers, semantic graph, macro family rankings, and macro cockpit artifacts.
Advisor outputs safe-now, simulate-first, review-first, gather-evidence, and avoid lists.
Advisor includes command lines users can run next without requiring global state or network.
Advisor refuses apply recommendations for repos with dirty worktrees unless explicitly allowed and rollback proof exists.

todos.task: Rank macro families by observed usefulness, safety, runtime, rollback reliability, and cross-repo generality.
acceptance: Ranker consumes outcome ledger, accuracy scorecard, benchmark corpus labels, and plugin adoption reports.
Families receive value, safety, generality, maturity, and promotion scores.
Ranker identifies families that should remain review-only or be retired.
Adoption cockpit shows the top useful macro families for the target repo.

todos.task: Create a typed Macro Operator IR that models every macro as an optimization operator over semantic graph nodes and repository files.
acceptance: IR includes operator id, input graph selectors, output graph effects, write-set effects, preconditions, postconditions, affected tests, rollback scope, and score factors.
Existing macro packs can be normalized into Macro Operator IR without losing v3/v4 contract evidence.
Operators distinguish review-only, simulate-capable, and apply-capable modes.
Validation rejects operators with ambiguous write effects, missing rollback scope, or untyped graph selectors.

todos.task: Record every macro simulation, drill, apply, rollback, review decision, and benchmark result into a local outcome ledger.
acceptance: Ledger records operator id, repo fingerprint, motif id, decision, score delta, runtime, affected tests, rollback result, review reason, and final status.
Ledger entries are append-only, hash-linked, and redactable for external repo use.
Outcome records can be generated from existing run logs and benchmark artifacts.
The ledger never stores secrets or full source content.

todos.task: Implement deterministic macro plan search that selects the best sequence or parallel stages of macro operators under score, risk, runtime, and rollback budgets.
acceptance: Search considers expected value, evidence confidence, dynamic risk, test cost, rollback cost, ownership risk, and composition constraints.
Search emits selected, rejected, dominated, deferred, and gather-evidence candidates with reasons.
Plans are deterministic across repeated runs and include stable hashes.
The confidence optimizer consumes plan search output instead of ranking isolated macro candidates only.

todos.task: Create a promotion gate that upgrades synthesized macro templates to trusted macro packs only after deterministic evidence thresholds are met.
acceptance: Promotion requires labeled fixtures, operator IR validation, composition compatibility, drill success, affected-test recall, false-safe-apply zero, and positive score delta.
Promotion writes a signed local manifest entry with source template hash and proof bundle hash.
Failed promotion explains missing evidence and required next fixtures.
Release SLO blocks trusted macro packs that lack promotion records.

todos.task: Emit a macro proof bundle that packages all evidence needed to review, replay, or audit a macro plan.
acceptance: Proof bundle includes operator IR, motif evidence, preconditions, composition report, plan search, drill report, affected tests, diffs, rollback manifest, and score delta proof.
Bundle has a stable content hash and can be replay-verified without network access.
Markdown rendering summarizes safe-now, review-first, and blocked reasons for humans.
Release SLO fails if apply-capable macro plans lack proof bundles.

todos.task: Synthesize reusable macro templates from clusters of motif candidates while keeping them review-only until fixtures and drills prove safety.
acceptance: Templates include parameters, graph selector schema, code edit adapter requirements, fixtures required, expected score delta, and promotion criteria.
Template synthesis deduplicates repeated candidates into reusable macro families.
Generated templates cannot be apply-capable until operator IR validation, preconditions, drill coverage, and benchmark thresholds pass.
Reports distinguish hand-authored packs, synthesized templates, and promoted templates.

todos.task: Create a transactional macro executor that applies composed macro plans with workspace isolation, staged commits, rollback checkpoints, resume support, and deterministic failure handling.
acceptance: Executor runs each stage in an isolated workspace before touching the target tree.
Every file change has before/after hash, rollback payload, formatter result, test selector, and run-log entry.
Partial failure triggers deterministic rollback or produces a resumable blocked state with exact failing stage.
Apply mode is impossible unless preconditions, composition, drills, policy, and affected tests all pass.

todos.task: Create a v1.2 macro release gate that proves the macro engine is safe, useful, compositional, transactional, and benchmark-backed.
acceptance: Gate reads operator IR, motif discovery, template synthesis, composition, plan search, transaction logs, proof bundles, outcome ledger, family rankings, promotion report, macro gauntlet, cockpit, and advisor artifacts.
Gate fails on false-safe-apply, rollback failure, non-idempotency, missing proof bundle, unpromoted trusted pack, missed affected tests, or macro gauntlet regression.
Readiness output includes stable hashes for decisive macro artifacts and clear remediation steps.
Plugin package manifest includes v1.2 macro readiness summary when present.

todos.task: Research mainstream AI coding tools and primary empirical evidence, identify cross-product pain points, and map each gap to a deterministic macro-wisdom capability that this project can implement and gate.
acceptance: Research covers at least six mainstream tools and uses versioned public sources with explicit claim provenance.
Every pain point maps to a deterministic macro response, concrete artifacts, and a measurable success criterion.
Vendor documentation establishes product behavior while surveys and primary studies establish cross-product pain; inferred gaps are labeled as inference.
The report is reproducible offline, keeps AI as a leaf worker, is exposed through the plugin and MCP, and is required by the v1.2 release gate.
